author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

On the words of the Gospel, John ix. 4 and 31, ‘We must work the works of him that sent me,’ etc. Against the Arians. And of that which the man who was born blind and received his sight said, ‘We know that God heareth not sinners.’

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, John ix. 4 and 31, ‘We must work the works of him that sent me,’ etc. Against the Arians. And of that which the man who was born blind and received his sight said, ‘We know that God heareth not sinners.’

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord Jesus, as we heard when the Holy Gospel was being read, opened the eyes of a man who was born blind.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Brethren, if we consider our hereditary punishment, the whole world is blind.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And therefore came Christ the Enlightener, because the devil had been the Blinder.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He made all men to be born blind, who seduced the first man.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them run to the Enlightener, let them run, believe, receive the clay made of the spittle.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Word is as it were the spittle, the Flesh is the earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them wash the face in the pool of Siloa.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now it was the Evangelist’s place to explain to us what Siloa means, and he said, “which is by interpretation, Sent.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who is This That is Sent, but He who in this very Lesson said, “I am come to do the works of Him That sent Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, Siloa, wash the face, be baptized, that ye may be enlightened, and that ye who before saw not, may see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, first open your eyes to that which is said; “I am come,” saith He, “to do the works of Him That sent Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now here at once stands forth the Arian, and says, “Here you see that Christ did not His Own works, but the Father’s who sent Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Would he say this, if he saw, that is, if he had washed his face in Him who was sent, as it were in Siloa?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then dost thou say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Lo,” says he, “Himself said it.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What said He?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “I am come to do the works of Him That sent Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Are they not then His Own?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: No. What then is that which the Siloa Himself saith, the Sent Himself, the Son Himself, the Only Son Himself, whom thou complainest of as degenerate?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is that He saith, “All things that the Father hath are Mine.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You say that He did the works of Another, in that He said, “I must do the works of Him That sent Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I say that the Father had the things of another: I am speaking according to your principles.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why would you object to me that Christ said, “I am come to do His works” as if, “not Mine own but ‘His That sent Me’”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I ask Thee, O Lord Christ, resolve the difficulty, put an end to the contention.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “All things,” saith He, “that the Father hath are Mine.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Are they then not the Father’s, if they are Thine?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He doth not say, “All things that the Father hath He hath given unto Me;” although, if He had said even this, He would have shown His equality.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the difficulty is that He said, “All things that the Father hath are Mine.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If you understand it aright, All things that the Father hath, are the Son’s; all things that the Son hath, are the Father’s.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear Him in another place; “All Mine are Thine, and Thine are Mine.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The question is finished, as to the things which the Father and the Son have: they have them with one consent, do not thou introduce dissension.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What He calleth the works of the Father, are His Own works; for, “Thine too are Mine,” for He speaketh of the works of That Father, to whom He said, “All Mine are Thine, and Thine are Mine.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then, My works are Thine, and Thy works are Mine.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For what things soever the Father doeth;” Himself hath said, the Lord hath said, the Only-Begotten hath said, the Son hath said, the Truth hath said.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What hath He said?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “What things soever the Father doeth, these also doeth the Son in like manner.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Signal expression! signal truth! signal equality.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “All things that the Father doeth, these doeth the Son also.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Were it enough to say, “All things that the Father doeth, these doeth the Son also”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is not enough; I add, “in like manner.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why do I add, “in like manner”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because they who do not understand, and who walk with eyes not yet open, are wont to say, “The Father doeth them by way of command, the Son of obedience, therefore not in like manner.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if in like manner, as the One, so the Other; so what things the One, the same the Other.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But,” says he, “the Father commands, that the Son may execute.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Carnal indeed is thy conceit, but without prejudice to the truth, I grant it to you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, the Father commands, the Son obeys; is the Son therefore not of the same Nature, because the One commands, and the Other obeys?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give me two men, father and son; they are two men: he that commands is a man; he that obeys is a man; he that commands and he that obeys have one and the same nature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Does not he that commands, beget a son of his own nature?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Does he who obeys, by obeying lose his nature?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now take for the present, as you thus take two men, the Father commanding, the Son obeying, yet God and God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But the first two together are two men, the Latter together is but One God; this is a divine miracle.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Meanwhile if you would that with you I acknowledge the obedience, do you first with me acknowledge the Nature.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father begat That which Himself is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If the Father begat ought else than what Himself is, He did not beget a true Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Father saith to the Son, “From the womb before the day-star, I begat Thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “before the day-star”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By the day-star times are signified.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then before times, before all that is called “before;” before all that is not, or before all that is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the Gospel does not say, “In the beginning God made the Word;” as it is said, “In the beginning God made the Heaven and the earth;” or, “In the beginning was the Word born;” or, “In the beginning God begat the Word.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what says it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He was, He was, He was.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You hear, “He was;” believe.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So often do ye hear, “Was:” seek not for time, for that He always “was.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then who always was, and was always with the Son, for that God is able to beget without thee; He said to the Son, “From the womb before the day-star I begat Thee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is from the womb?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Had God a womb?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Shall we imagine that God was fashioned with bodily members?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And why said He, “From the womb,” but that it might be understood that He begat Him of His Own Substance?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then from the womb came forth That which Himself was who begat.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if He who begat was one thing, and another came forth out of the womb; it were a monster, not a Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore let the Son do the works of Him That sent Him, and the Father also do the works of the Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “At all events,” you say, “the Father wills, the Son executes.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, I show, that the Son willeth, and the Father executeth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do you say, “where dost thou show this?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I show it at once.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Father, I will.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now here if I had a mind to cavil, lo, the Son commandeth, and the Father executeth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What wilt Thou?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That where I am, they may be also with Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We have escaped, there shall we be, where He is; there shall we be, we have escaped.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who can undo the “I Will” of the Almighty?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You hear the will of His power, hear now the power of His will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “As the Father” saith He “raiseth up the dead and quickeneth them; even so the Son quickeneth whom He will.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Whom He will.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Say not, The Son quickeneth them, whom the Father commandeth Him to quicken.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He quickeneth whom He will.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then whom the Father will, and whom Himself will: because where there is One Power, there is One Will.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us then in a heart blind no more hold fast that the Nature of the Father and the Son is One and the Same; because the Father is very Father, the Son is very Son.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What He is, That did He beget: because the Begotten was not degenerate.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is a something in the words of that man who was blind, which may cause perplexity, and peradventure make many who understand them not aright despair.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For he said amongst the rest of his words, the same man whose eyes were opened, “We know that God heareth not sinners.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What shall we do, if God heareth not sinners?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dare we pray to God if He heareth not sinners?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give me one who may pray: lo, here is One to hear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give me one who may pray, sift thoroughly the human race from the imperfect to the perfect.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Mount up from the spring to the summer; for this we have just chanted.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thou hast made summer and spring;” that is, “Those who are already spiritual, and those who are still carnal hast Thou made;” for so the Son Himself saith, “Thine Eyes have seen My imperfect being.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That which is imperfect in My Body, Thine Eyes have seen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what then?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Have they who are imperfect hope?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Undoubtedly they have.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear what follows; “And in Thy Book shall all be written.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But perhaps, Brethren, the spiritual pray and are heard, because they are not sinners?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then must the carnal do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What must they do?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Shall they perish?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Shall they not pray to God?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Give me that publican in the Gospel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Come, thou publican, stand forth, show thy hope, that the weak may not lose hope.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For behold the publican went up with the Pharisee to pray, and with face cast down upon the ground, standing afar off, beating his breast, he said, “Lord, be merciful to me a sinner.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And he went down justified rather than the Pharisee.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Said he true or false, who said, “Be merciful to me a sinner”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If he said true, he was a sinner; yet was he heard and justified.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then is that, that thou whose eyes the Lord opened didst say, “We know that God heareth not sinners”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, God doth hear sinners.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But wash thou thy inferior face, let that be done in thy heart, which hath been done in thy face; and thou wilt see that God doth hear sinners.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The imagination of thine heart hath deceived thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is still something for Him to do to thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We see that this man was cast out of the synagogue; Jesus heard of it, came to him, and said to him, “Dost thou believe on the Son of God?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And He said, “Who is He, Lord, that I should believe on Him?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He saw, and did not see; he saw with the eyes, but as yet with the heart he saw not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord said to him, “Thou both seest Him,” that is, with the eyes; “and He that talketh with thee is He.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He then fell down, and worshipped Him.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then washed he the face of his heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Apply yourselves then earnestly to prayer, ye sinners: confess your sins, pray that they may be blotted out, pray that they may be diminished, pray that as ye increase, they may decrease: yet do not despair, and sinners though ye be, pray.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For who hath not sinned?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Begin with the priests.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: To the priests it is said, “First offer sacrifices for your own sins, and so for the people.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The sacrifices convicted the priests that if any one should call himself righteous and without sin, it might be answered him, “I look not at what thou sayest, but at what thou offerest; thine own victim convicteth thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore dost thou offer for thine own sins, if thou have no sins?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Dost thou in thy sacrifice lie unto God?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But peradventure the priests of the ancient people were sinners; of the new people are not sinners.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of a truth, Brethren, for that God hath so willed, I am His priest; I am a sinner; with you do I beat the breast, with you I ask for pardon, with you I hope that God will be merciful.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But peradventure the Holy Apostles, those first and highest leaders of the flock, shepherds, members of The Shepherd, these peradventure had no sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yes, indeed, even they had, they had indeed; they are not angry at this, for they confess it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I should not dare.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: First hear the Lord Himself saying to the Apostles, “In this manner pray ye.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As those other priests were convicted by the sacrifices, so these by prayer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And amongst the other things which He commanded them to pray for, He appointed this also, “Forgive us our debts, as we also forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What do the Apostles say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Every day they pray for their debts to be forgiven them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They come in debtors, they go out absolved, and return debtors to prayer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This life is not without sin, that as often as prayer is made, so often should sins be forgiven.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what shall I say?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peradventure when they learnt the prayer, they were still weak.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Some one, perhaps, will say this.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When the Lord Jesus taught them that prayer, they were yet babes, weak, carnal; they were not yet spiritual, who have no sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then, Brethren?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When they became spiritual, did they cease to pray?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then Christ ought to have said, “Pray in such wise now;” and to have given them, when spiritual, another prayer.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is one and the same.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who gave it is One and the Same; use it then in prayer in the Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But we will take away all controversy, when you say the Holy Apostles were spiritual, up to the time of the Lord’s Passion they were carnal; this you must say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And indeed, the truth is, as He was hanging, they were in alarm, and the Apostles then despaired when the robber believed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Peter dared to follow, when the Lord was led to suffering, he dared to follow, who came to the house, and was wearied in the palace, and stood at the fire, and was cold; he stood at the fire, he was frozen with a chilling fear.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Being questioned by the maid-servant, he denied Christ once; being questioned a second time, he denied Him; being questioned a third time, he denied Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: God be thanked, that the questioning ceased; if the questioning had not ceased, long would the denial have been repeated.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then after He rose again, then He confirmed them, then did they become spiritual.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Had they at that time then no sin?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostles spiritual, wrote spiritual epistles, they sent them to the Churches; “they had no sin.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This you say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I do not believe you, I ask themselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tell us, O holy Apostles, after the Lord rose again, and confirmed you with the Holy Ghost sent from heaven; did ye cease to have sin?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Tell us, I pray you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us hear, that sinners may not despair, that they may not leave off to pray to God, because they are not without sin.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: One of them saith.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He whom the Lord loved the most, and who lay on the Lord’s Breast, and drank in the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven which he was to pour forth again.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Him I ask; “Have ye sin or not?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He maketh answer and saith, “If we shall say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now it is the same John who said, “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See ye what heights he had passed, that he could reach to the Word!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Such an one, and so great, who like an eagle soared above the clouds, who in the serene clearness of his mind saw, “In the beginning was the Word;” he hath said, “If we shall say that we have no sin, we deceive ourselves, and the truth is not in us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if we shall confess our sins, He is faithful and just to forgive us our sins, and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore pray ye.
